creator: stefan.hegnauer@gmx.ch
text:
> Can you perhaps tell us more about the setup you are having 
> with the jails? showing the devfs ruleset that is being used 
> for the jails etc?
> 
> Normally the /dev/pf node isn't visible in jails and this 
> shouldn't happen..
> 
> Thanks,
> Remko
> 
> 
> -- 
> /"\   Best regards,                        | remko@FreeBSD.org
> \ /   Remko Lodder                      | remko@EFnet
> X    http://www.evilcoder.org/    |
> / \   ASCII Ribbon Campaign    | Against HTML Mail and News

Sorry for the long delay in answering. I can attest now that the problem is
NOT with FreeBSD. 

Your questions about devfs and visibility of the pf node where spot on:
somehow the /etc/devfs.rules got an erroneous ruleset with number=1
(probably to allow for USB disks), basically overwriting the #1 rule of
/etc/defaults/devfs.rules. This in turn made the
'jail_xxxjail_devfs_ruleset="devfsrules_jail"' entries in /etc/rc.conf
useless resulting in full visibility not only of pf but all other nodes as
well - disks for example ...

After re-numbering the offending ruleset in /etc/devfs.rules and a reboot
everything is fine now. Call me an idiot, and please close the ticket
kern/130102! Thanks for your help, and sorry for the noise!!

Best regards,
Stefan
